message:
fix(slot): add cross-beads prefix routing to bd slot set (bd-hmeb)

When setting a slot to a bead from a different beads database
(e.g., setting an hq-* role bead on a gt-* agent bead), the command
now uses prefix-based routing via routes.jsonl to resolve the bead
in the correct database.

Previously, bd slot set only looked in the local database, failing
to find cross-db references like hq-polecat-role from rig beads.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-396j","title":"GetBlockedIssues shows external deps as blocking even when satisfied","description":"GetBlockedIssues (ready.go:385-493) shows external:* refs in the blocked_by list but doesn't check if they're actually satisfied using CheckExternalDep.

This can be confusing - an issue shows as blocked by external:project:capability even if that capability has been shipped (closed issue with provides: label exists).

Options:
1. Call CheckExternalDep for each external ref and filter satisfied ones from blocked_by
2. Add a note in output indicating external deps need lazy resolution
3. Document this is expected behavior (bd blocked shows all deps, bd ready shows resolved state)

Related: GetReadyWork correctly filters by external deps, but GetBlockedIssues doesn't.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-396j","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286971-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3bhl","title":"Add sync rollback on git commit failure","description":"Part of GH#885 fix: graceful failure handling.

When git commit fails after export:
1. Detect the failure
2. Roll back SQLite metadata to pre-export state
3. Optionally restore JSONL from git HEAD
4. Report clear error with recovery instructions

This makes sync failures recoverable without manual intervention.

Depends on the core atomicity fix - this is defense in depth.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:27.971284-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:27.971284-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bhl","depends_on_id":"bd-pnx2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:55.869715-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"},{"issue_id":"bd-3bhl","depends_on_id":"bd-usvv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:57.388449-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bsz","title":"gt mail send: support reading message body from stdin","description":"Currently gt mail send -m requires the message as a command-line argument, which causes shell escaping issues with backticks, quotes, and special characters.

Add support for reading message body from stdin:
- gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin  # Read body from stdin
- echo 'body' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' -m -  # Convention: -m - means stdin

This would allow:
cat <<'EOF' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin
Message with `backticks` and 'quotes' safely
EOF

Without this, agents struggle to send handoff messages containing code snippets.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00"}

Recommendation: Consider using a fixed reference time or time injection for deterministic tests. Lower priority since current margin is large. +{"id":"bd-hmeb","title":"bd slot set doesn't support cross-beads prefix routing","description":"## Problem

`bd slot set` fails when setting a slot to a bead that lives in a different beads database (cross-db routing).

## Evidence

When spawning a polecat:
```
Warning: could not set role slot: bd slot set gt-gastown-polecat-chumbucket role hq-polecat-role: 
Error: failed to resolve bead hq-polecat-role: no issue found matching "hq-polecat-role"
```

The agent bead `gt-gastown-polecat-chumbucket` is in rig beads (`gastown/mayor/rig/.beads/`).
The role bead `hq-polecat-role` is in town beads (`~/gt/.beads/`).

`bd show hq-polecat-role` works fine (uses prefix routing via routes.jsonl).
`bd slot set ... hq-polecat-role` fails (does not use routing).

## Root Cause

`bd slot set` resolves the target bead in the current database only, ignoring the `hq-` prefix that should route to town beads.

## Expected Behavior

`bd slot set <agent> <slot> <bead>` should:
1. Check if `<bead>` has a routable prefix (e.g., `hq-`, `gt-`, `bd-`)
2. If so, resolve it via routes.jsonl like `bd show` does
3. Store the bead reference (cross-db references are valid for slots)

## Workaround

Currently the warning is non-fatal - the role slot just is not set. But this breaks role-based queries.

## Files

- `cmd/slot.go` - slot set command implementation
- Need to add routing logic similar to `cmd/show.go`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/garnet","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:12:40.230597-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:19.493134-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:19.493134-08:00","close_reason":"Added prefix routing to bd slot set command. Now uses needsRouting() and resolveAndGetIssueWithRouting() to handle cross-beads references like hq-* from rig beads."}
 {"id":"bd-hnkg","title":"GH#540: Add silent quick-capture mode (bd q)","description":"Add bd q alias for quick capture that outputs only issue ID. Useful for piping/scripting. See GitHub issue #540.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:03:38.260135-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"feature"}
 {"id":"bd-hobo","title":"Distinct prefixes for protos, molecules, wisps","description":"Template/workflow entities should have visually distinct prefixes from regular issues.

**Problem:**
- Protos (bd-7bs4) look like regular issues - invites squashing
- Molecules (poured instances) also use bd- prefix
- Wisps are in separate DB but still use bd- when referenced

**Proposed Prefixes:**
- `proto-` for templates (e.g., proto-release, proto-review)
- `mol-` for active molecules (poured from protos)
- `wisp-` for ephemeral wisps (vapor phase)

**Benefits:**
- Instant visual recognition of entity type
- Prevents accidental modification of templates
- Clear lifecycle: proto → mol → wisp → digest

**Implementation options:**
1. Separate databases with different prefixes
2. Issue type determines prefix generation
3. Naming convention enforced by bd pour/wisp commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","labels":["workflow"]}
 {"id":"bd-hp8g","title":"gt sling should look up formulas from .beads/formulas/ registry","description":"## Problem

gt sling <formula-name> doesn't find formulas from the registry that bd formula list shows.

## What I tried

1. gt sling beads/crew/emma --formula beads-release --var version=0.42.0
   - Problem: --formula flag doesn't exist

2. gt sling beads-release beads/crew/emma --var version=0.42.0
   - Error: open .../beads-release: no such file or directory
   - Problem: Looks for file in cwd, not formula registry

## What worked

bd mol wisp beads-release --var version=0.42.0 - correctly finds formula from .beads/formulas/

## Expected

gt sling should use the same formula lookup as bd mol wisp - checking .beads/formulas/*.formula.json

## Documentation gap

The help shows examples like 'gt sling mol-release mayor/' suggesting formula slinging works, but it doesn't find registry formulas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T21:18:17.251292-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:09:08.199761-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T22:06:27.153176-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-posw","title":"hq- prefix routing fails: '.' resolves relative to cwd, not town root","description":"## Problem
The hq- prefix in routes.jsonl maps to '.' which is interpreted relative to the current working directory, not the town root.

## Reproduction
```bash
cd ~/gt && bd show hq-2cchm    # Works
cd ~/gt/beads/crew/emma && bd show hq-2cchm  # Fails: 'no issue found'
```

## Root Cause
routes.jsonl has: {"prefix": "hq-", "path": "."}

The '.' is resolved relative to cwd, so from crew/emma it looks for ~/gt/beads/crew/emma/.beads/ instead of ~/gt/.beads/

## Fix
The routing logic should resolve '.' as the town root, or the route should use an absolute path or a sentinel like 'TOWN_ROOT'.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T12:31:37.258235-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-29T13:45:37.471123-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T13:45:37.471123-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed by adding town-aware route resolution"}

 {"id":"bd-umbf","title":"Design contributor namespace isolation for beads pollution prevention","description":"## Problem

When contributors work on beads-the-project using beads-the-tool, their personal work-tracking issues leak into PRs. The .beads/issues.jsonl is intentionally tracked (it's the project's issue database), but contributors' local issues pollute the diff.

This is a recursion problem unique to self-hosting projects.

## Possible Solutions to Explore

1. **Contributor namespaces** - Each contributor gets a private prefix (e.g., `bd-steve-xxxx`) that's gitignored or filtered
2. **Separate database** - Contributors use BEADS_DIR pointing elsewhere for personal tracking
3. **Issue ownership/visibility flags** - Mark issues as "local-only" vs "project"
4. **Prefix-based filtering** - Configure which prefixes are committed vs ignored

## Design Considerations

- Should be zero-friction for contributors (no manual setup)
- Must not break existing workflows
- Needs to work with sync/collaboration features
- Consider: what if a "personal" issue graduates to "project" issue?

## Expansion Needed

This is a placeholder. Needs detailed design exploration before implementation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:29.638743-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T18:15:53.217813-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T17:05:13.732967-08:00"}

+{"id":"bd-usvv","title":"Defer SQLite metadata updates until after git commit succeeds","description":"Part of GH#885 fix: bulletproof sync atomicity.

Currently exportToJSONL() immediately:
- Clears dirty flags in SQLite
- Updates last_import_time metadata

This happens BEFORE git commit, so if commit fails, SQLite "lies" about sync state.

Fix: Move metadata updates to AFTER git commit succeeds:
1. Export JSONL to disk (atomic file write)
2. Stage and commit to git
3. ONLY THEN clear dirty flags and update last_import_time

Key files:
- cmd/bd/sync_export.go:147-170 (premature updates)
- cmd/bd/sync.go:555 (commit point)

This is the core fix for the atomicity gap.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:23.619319-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:18:11.715019-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:21.245756-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-usvv","depends_on_id":"bd-pnx2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T13:53:55.798447-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uu8p","title":"Routing bypassed in daemon mode","description":"## Bug

Prefix-based routing (routes.jsonl) only works in direct mode. When the daemon is running, it bypasses routing logic because:

1. Daemon mode resolves IDs via RPC (`daemonClient.ResolveID`)
2. Daemon uses its own store, which only knows about local beads
3. The routing logic in `resolveAndGetIssueWithRouting` is only called in direct mode

## Reproduction

```bash
# With daemon running:
cd ~/gt
bd show gt-1py3y  # Fails - daemon can't find it

# With daemon bypassed:
bd --no-daemon show gt-1py3y  # Works - uses routing
```

## Fix Options

### Option A: Client-side routing detection (Recommended)
Before calling daemon RPC, check if the ID prefix matches a route to a different beads dir. If so, use direct mode with routing for that ID.

```go
// In show.go, before daemon mode:
if daemonClient != nil && needsRouting(id) {
    // Fall back to direct mode for this ID
    result, err := resolveAndGetIssueWithRouting(ctx, store, id)
    ...
}
```

### Option B: Daemon-side routing
Add routing support to the daemon RPC server. More complex - daemon would need to:
- Load routes.jsonl on startup
- Open connections to multiple databases
- Route requests based on ID prefix

### Option C: Hybrid
Daemon returns "not found + prefix hint", client retries with routing.

## Recommendation

Option A is simplest. The client already has routing logic; just use it when we detect a routed prefix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-26T14:52:00.452285-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T14:54:51.572289-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T14:54:51.572289-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-uwkp","title":"Phase 2.4: Git merge driver optimization for TOON format","description":"Optimize git 3-way merge for TOON line-oriented format.

## Overview
TOON is line-oriented (unlike binary formats), enabling smarter git merge strategies. Implement custom merge driver to handle TOON-specific merge patterns.

## Required Work

### 2.4.1 TOON Merge Driver
- [ ] Create .git/info/attributes entry for *.toon files
- [ ] Implement custom merge driver script/command
- [ ] Handle tabular format row merges (line-based 3-way)
- [ ] Handle YAML-style format merges
- [ ] Conflict markers for unsolvable conflicts

### 2.4.2 Merge Patterns
- [ ] Row addition: both branches add different rows → union
- [ ] Row deletion: one branch deletes, other modifies → conflict (manual review)
- [ ] Row modification: concurrent field changes → intelligent merge or conflict
- [ ] Field ordering changes: ignore (TOON format resilient to order)

### 2.4.3 Testing & Documentation
- [ ] Unit tests for merge scenarios (3-way merge logic)
- [ ] Integration tests with actual git merges
- [ ] Conflict scenario testing
- [ ] Documentation of merge strategy

## Success Criteria
- Git merge handles TOON conflicts intelligently
- Fewer manual merge conflicts than JSONL
- Round-trip preserved through merges
- All 70+ tests still passing
- Git history stays clean (minimal conflict markers)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.339238776-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:26.434306-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:26.434306-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uwkp","depends_on_id":"bd-iic1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:14.34427988-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

This is independent of the filter change but complementary.","notes":"## Implementation Plan

### Code Changes (cmd/bd/list.go)

1. **Change default limit** (init function, ~line 887):
```go
// Currently: listCmd.Flags().IntP("limit", "n", 0, "Limit results")
// Change to:
listCmd.Flags().IntP("limit", "n", 50, "Limit results (0 for unlimited)")
```

2. **Update help text**:
```go
"Limit results (default: 50, use 0 for unlimited)"
```

3. **Handle explicit 0** in Run function:
```go
// --limit 0 means unlimited
if cmd.Flags().Changed("limit") && limit == 0 {
    filter.Limit = 0 // No limit applied
} else if limit > 0 {
    filter.Limit = limit
} else {
    filter.Limit = 50 // Default
}
```

### Testing
- `bd list` shows max 50 issues
- `bd list --limit 10` shows max 10
- `bd list --limit 0` shows unlimited
- Verify output indicates truncation: "Showing 50 of 499 issues (use --limit 0 for all)"

### Documentation
- Note in help: default changed from unlimited to 50","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:08.299726-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-29T17:53:30.23005-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T17:53:30.23005-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in single commit","labels":["gh:788"]}

 {"id":"bd-vii9","title":"bd agent state fails when agent bead doesn't exist","description":"## Bug

`bd agent state <agent> <state>` fails if the agent doesn't already exist as a bead.

## Reproduction

```
gt sling gt-nrpiq gastown
...
Warning: couldn't update agent gt-gastown-polecat-toast hook to gt-nrpiq: 
  updating agent state: bd agent state gt-gastown-polecat-toast running: 
  Error: failed to resolve agent gt-gastown-polecat-toast: 
  no issue found matching "gt-gastown-polecat-toast"
```

## Context from HOP Design

From **docs/hop/CONTEXT.md**:
> "Each polecat should have a chain: What work have they done? What's their success rate? What skills have they demonstrated?"

From **docs/PRIMING.md**:
> "Polecats are ephemeral executors. Like K8s pods. They have names (for debugging, logs) but no persistent identity."

**Resolution**: Polecat *sessions* are ephemeral, but polecat *names* (toast, nux, furiosa, etc.) are recycled and SHOULD have persistent identity beads. The name is the identity - sessions come and go.

Think of it like K8s:
- **Pod** = polecat session (ephemeral, killed after task)
- **ServiceAccount** = polecat identity bead (persistent, accumulates history)

## Solution

`bd agent state` should **auto-create** the agent bead on first use:

```go
// If agent bead doesn't exist, create it
if errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound) {
    // Create identity bead for this agent
    _, err = CreateIssue(Issue{
        Title:    fmt.Sprintf("Agent: %s", agentName),
        Type:     "agent",
        Status:   state,
        Metadata: map[string]string{
            "role": parseRole(agentName),  // polecat, witness, etc.
            "rig":  parseRig(agentName),   // gastown, beads, etc.
        },
    })
}
```

This enables:
1. Work history accumulation per polecat name
2. Skill/success tracking over time
3. CV chain for the polecat identity
4. `bd list --type=agent` to see all agents

## Non-goals

- Session tracking (ephemeral, don't persist)
- Work attribution to polecat (credits human owner)
- Agent scheduling (Gas Town handles that)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","created_at":"2026-01-02T12:16:39.116652-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-02T12:27:05.679183-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T12:27:05.679183-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented auto-create logic in runAgentState to create agent beads on first use"}

 {"id":"bd-vks2","title":"bd dep tree doesn't display external dependencies","description":"GetDependencyTree (dependencies.go:464-624) uses a recursive CTE that JOINs with the issues table, which means external refs (external:project:capability) are invisible in the tree output.

When an issue has an external blocking dependency, running 'bd dep tree <id>' won't show it.

Options:
1. Query dependencies table separately for external refs and display them as leaf nodes
2. Add a synthetic 'external' node type that shows the ref and resolution status
3. Document that external deps aren't shown in tree view (use bd show for full deps)

Lower priority since bd show <id> displays all dependencies including external refs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:27.121934-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:30:19.083652-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:30:19.083652-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vks2","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:27.122511-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-yuxq","title":"Code smell: 262 uses of interface{} reduce type safety","description":"Heavy use of interface{} across 92 files (262 occurrences) in:
- RPC update arguments: updatesFromArgs() returns map[string]interface{}
- JSON unmarshaling and formatting
- Storage query helpers

**Example in internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go:45:**
```go
func updatesFromArgs(a UpdateArgs) map[string]interface{} {
    // Returns map with interface{} values - types must be asserted at use sites
}
```

**Problem:**
- Loss of type safety
- Runtime type assertions required
- Harder to catch errors at compile time

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Define typed update structs for specific field groups
- [ ] Use generics where possible (Go 1.18+)
- [ ] Reduce interface{} count by at least 50%
- [ ] Document expected types where interface{} remains necessary
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:01.896147-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:55:53.203941-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:04:41.376731-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yuxq","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.812412-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-z4f5","title":"--parent flag reports success but doesn't persist to JSONL","description":"The --parent flag for bd update reports success but the change doesn't persist.

Reported by: Mayor
Location: show.go:810-814 handles the logic

Symptoms:
- bd update <id> --parent=<new-parent> returns success
- But the parent change is not written to JSONL
- Subsequent bd show reveals parent unchanged

This shipped in v0.39.1 as part of bd-cj2e (--parent flag for reparenting).

Investigation: Check the update path from show.go through to JSONL export. The RPC/daemon layer may be dropping the parent field, or the storage layer isn't persisting it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T23:10:22.428136-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-27T23:27:45.716572-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T23:27:45.716572-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-z6kw","title":"Refinery gh:run gate auto-discovery","description":"Refinery auto-populates await_id for gh:run gates from recent workflow runs.

## Problem
Gate created by cook has await_type=gh:run but no await_id.
Need to discover the run ID from the push that triggered CI.

## Solution
During Refinery patrol:
1. Find open gh:run gates without await_id
2. Query: gh run list --workflow=<gate.workflow> --limit=5
3. Match run to gate (by branch, commit, or time proximity)
4. Update gate: bd update <gate-id> --await-id=<run-id>
5. Now bd gate check --type=gh can poll that specific run

## Implementation
- Add discover-await-ids step to mol-refinery-patrol
- Use gh CLI to query recent runs
- Match heuristics: same branch, recent commit, within 5 mins
- Update gate with discovered run ID

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Refinery discovers run IDs for gh:run gates
- [ ] Gates updated with correct await_id
- [ ] Subsequent polls use run ID
- [ ] Handles multiple pending gates","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:42.35667-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-04T11:25:30.602391-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T16:12:24.656236-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z6kw","depends_on_id":"bd-d1n1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.621422-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-z6kw","depends_on_id":"bd-4k3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.688305-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}
